description: Three-key trust hierarchy for verifying devices and users. Master key signs self-signing and user-signing keys. All uploads are cryptographically validated before storage.

actors.name: Key Owner
actors.description: User uploading or updating their cross-signing keys

actors.name: Verifying User
actors.description: User checking another user's key signatures to establish trust

actors.name: Homeserver
actors.description: Server validating cryptographic signatures and storing keys

fields.name: master_key
fields.label: Root key for the account; all other keys must be signed by this key

fields.name: self_signing_key
fields.label: Key used to sign the owner's own device keys; must be signed by master_key

fields.name: user_signing_key
fields.label: Key used to sign other users' master keys (trust delegation); must be signed by master_key

fields.name: user_id
fields.label: Account the cross-signing keys belong to

fields.name: signatures
fields.label: Cryptographic signatures on each key proving its authority chain

key_hierarchy: All three key types must be signed by the master key before they can be stored
A master key must already exist before other keys can be uploaded, or all three can be uploaded together
Uploading a new master key invalidates all existing device verifications for the account
Self-signing keys sign the owner's own device keys
User-signing keys sign other users' master keys for trust delegation
notifications: Master or self-signing key updates trigger device list update notifications to all users in shared rooms
User-signing key updates only notify the key owner (not broadcast to room members)
All signature verification uses canonical JSON serialization

given: all submitted keys carry valid signatures from the master key

any: master key is being uploaded alongside other keys
master key already exists on the server

then.description: Cross-signing keys persisted to key store

keys_uploaded.result: Keys are stored and trust hierarchy is established

given: master key differs from the existing one

then.description: New master key stored; prior device verifications invalidated
Device list update broadcast to all users in shared encrypted rooms

master_key_updated.result: Account's root of trust is rotated; remote devices are notified

given: new user-signing key carries valid master key signature

then.description: Signature update notification sent only to the key owner

user_signing_key_updated.result: Key owner can now sign other users' master keys to extend trust

given: target user's keys are available locally or via federation
keys_queried.result: Requester receives target user's cross-signing key bundle

any: master key signature fails cryptographic verification
self-signing key is not validly signed by master key
user-signing key is not validly signed by master key

invalid_signature.result: Key upload rejected; no keys stored

given: self-signing or user-signing key uploaded without a master key
no master key exists on the server for this account
master_key_missing.result: Upload rejected; master key must be established first

errors.message: One or more cross-signing keys have invalid signatures
A master key must be provided before uploading other cross-signing keys
No cross-signing keys found for this user

events.name: cross_signing.keys_uploaded
events.description: One or more cross-signing keys were successfully stored

events.name: cross_signing.master_key_updated
events.description: The account's master key was replaced; device verifications may need renewal

events.name: cross_signing.user_signing_key_updated
events.description: The user-signing key was updated; only the key owner is notified

events.name: cross_signing.keys_retrieved
events.description: A user's cross-signing key bundle was returned to a requester

related.reason: Cross-signing keys are bundled with device keys in key query responses
Cross-signing updates trigger device list broadcasts; device deletions affect key associations

goals.description: Three-key trust hierarchy for verifying devices and users. Master key signs self-signing and user-signing keys. All uploads are cryptographically validated before storage.

tradeoffs.reason: authentication must prioritize preventing unauthorized access

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details
